message:
🐛 fix: GlobeAnimation falls back gracefully when WebGL init is delayed (#8004) (#8042)

Root cause: GlobeAnimation detected WebGL once at module-eval time using
`useState(isWebGLAvailable)` and `instanceof WebGLRenderingContext`. In
containerized production deployments the GPU rendering context is briefly
unavailable while the page is hydrating and lazy chunks resolve, and the
`instanceof` check can return false even when WebGL is functional (vendor
context object vs. global constructor realm mismatch). Once that initial
detection fell through to `false`, the loader continued to render
indefinitely on top of the static fallback because the loader was only
gated on `!isLoaded`, never on the WebGL failure state.

Fix:
- Replace one-shot detection with a polled retry after mount
  (WEBGL_MAX_RETRIES * WEBGL_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS = 2s window).
- Drop the realm-fragile `instanceof` check in favor of a truthy
  context object test (matches three.js / @react-three/fiber behavior).
- Track three explicit WebGL states (checking / available / unavailable)
  and only show the loader while we are still checking or rendering;
  the static gradient fallback now actually appears when WebGL never
  becomes available.
- Guard against `document` being undefined (SSR safety).

All timing values are named constants with comments explaining the
total wait window.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
